topic: Part 8b, Section 8b.3 eclipse-layers audit — post-EXP-230 Fail-clause attribution fix
change_summary: EXP-230 integrated: two targeted substring replacements in the L3a Fail-clause double-bind paragraph. Patch A opened the block with 'Our codified Fail-clause — the phrase … that we appended to our Pass band' (plus a parenthetical citing PRED-095 as registered containing no Chapman-language Fail criterion and asserting 'any fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below'). Patch B closed the block with 'The dome has not committed to either disambiguation' in place of 'The dome does not publish which reading governs.' Intent: resolve ISS-1422 (c5 Hole 1 moderate) — attribution misframing that treated the Chapman-language Fail-clause as a dome concession rather than our steel-man codification. The interpretation-A and interpretation-B sentences between the two anchors are unchanged; the upstream threshold-codification paragraph (which already labeled the band 'our Pass band') is unchanged; no edits to 8b.1, 8b.2, or 8b.4.
our_argument_summary: Under adoption of the dome author's fair-audit proposal, we restructure eclipse content into a four-layer stack (L1 ensemble anchor WIN-068, L2 Mohe timing WIN-011, L3a magnetic falsifier PRED-095, L3b gravity PRED-TIER-2, L4 optics caveat) and publish a codified magnetic Tier 3 threshold. Two structural critiques survive adoption: (i) an ensemble 'anchor' fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor (OTS-wrong-side pattern in physics form); (ii) a magnitude-only Pass band does not distinguish dome from globe because the arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment of the same observational corpus. PRED-095's derivation is not absent; it is globe-parasitic by construction (WIN-025 globe-fingerprint + the dome author's own OPEN-001 coordinate admission). The post-EXP-224 double-bind paragraph argues that the appended 'signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone' Fail-clause admits two readings and the Pass band fails under either. EXP-230 now owns that Fail-clause codification as ours and reroutes the disambiguation burden to the dome's unspecified falsifier.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome publishes per-station predictions with dual baselines (BOU 2017 −10.9 nT, W004 empirical −22.24 nT) × coverage × FSF, plus four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D). Our tracker records PRED-095 as a single amplitude-band claim ('−17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations'). Our codified Pass band with appended 'signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone' Fail-clause is an auditor-side steel-man on top of the dome's amplitude prediction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Mixed. The FSF comes from V12 H(r)/r dome geometry (dome-derived). Baselines BOU 2017 and W004 are INTERMAGNET globe-measured values (globe-parasitic). Coverage fractions are Besselian-element computations assuming a spherical Earth. The section correctly labels PRED-095 as globe-parasitic by construction.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET magnetometer network (BOU, W004, HAD, EBR, etc.)
Van Camp et al. 2001 (via WIN-011 cite)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Per-station tolerances (±1-3 nT) implicit in the dome's E001-E009 table; no explicit Chapman-mechanism null-test criterion; no explicit rule for handling site-level heterogeneity within the W004 −17 to −21 nT cluster. The new parenthetical correctly records the absence of a Chapman-language Fail criterion in PRED-095 as registered.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by EXP-230. raw-text/03-predictions.txt corroborates data/predictions.json — the dome's registered prediction specifies per-station amplitude bands and four named sub-tests (E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D), but contains no Chapman-mechanism null-test or Sq-dynamo Fail-clause. The section's 'PRED-095 as registered in data/predictions.json contains no Chapman-language Fail criterion at all' is corroborated by the dome's own predictions.html raw text.

code_analysis_tags.note: SEC-* items don't carry WIN-style code_analysis_tags. The eclipse-layer audit inherits WIN-068.derives_from_dome:false and WIN-025.relabels_standard:true via explicit cross-refs. Those tags are accurate as cited.

holes_found.description: Unscoped overclaim in the new parenthetical: 'so any fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below.' A dome defender can legitimately reply that the ambiguity is a property of *our chosen Fail-clause wording* ('signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone'), not of fair-audit Fail-clauses in general. The dome's own page publishes four explicitly model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, E-PRED-D non-path stations below noise floor — verified in raw-text/03-predictions.txt). An auditor could construct a non-ambiguous Fail-clause built around E-PRED-B ('Fail = Hartland peak ΔZ < Ebro peak ΔZ in magnitude') that does not inherit the Chapman/Sq reading ambiguity, and the double-bind evaporates under that Fail-clause. The section acknowledges the sub-tests exist but bypasses them with 'do not change the non-discrimination assessment of the magnitude-only W004 cluster' — which is correct as a scope-limit on magnitude-only tests, but the sentence 'any fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below' does not carry that scope-limit. A defender reading only the double-bind paragraph will see the unscoped 'any' and treat it as overreach.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten scope of the parenthetical. Replace 'so any fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below' with 'so any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below' — or more precisely 'so any fair-audit Fail-clause framed against the ionospheric Sq baseline inherits the ambiguity below.' The scope-narrowing forecloses the E-PRED-B-style defender rejoinder and preserves the forced-disjunction structure for the magnitude-only case the section is arguing about. This is a one-word insertion and should not widen the anchor beyond the sentence-level substring.

holes_found.description: 'appended to our Pass band' is slightly imprecise. The threshold-codification paragraph upstream quotes the Pass band as 'observed −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations, ≥3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments; Fail = outside this range, or signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone' — the Sq-variation clause is *included in* our codification from the outset, not added later. 'Appended' implies a later addition or bolt-on. A precise-minded defender could argue that if the clause was there from the start, the ambiguity was baked into our codification from the start and we should own the whole band, not just the Fail-tail. The semantic slip is small and the attribution-owning intent is preserved, but 'embedded within our Pass band' or 'included in our Pass band' would be more accurate.
holes_found.recommendation: Swap 'appended to our Pass band' for 'included in our Pass band' or 'embedded within our Pass band.' One-word precision fix; no structural change.

holes_found.description: 'Chapman-language Fail criterion' is jargon that a first-time reader has to reverse-engineer. The phrase means 'a Fail criterion that invokes the Chapman Sq-dynamo mechanism or explicitly targets ionospheric-conductivity-driven eclipse modulation.' A non-specialist reader will not unpack that from the parenthetical alone; the upstream Chapman references are several paragraphs earlier and are not visually linked. This is a readability minor, not an argument flaw — the specialist audience for this section can parse it, and adding an inline gloss risks inflating sentence length further.
holes_found.recommendation: Leave as-is for specialist audience, OR swap 'Chapman-language Fail criterion' for 'mechanism-level Fail criterion (e.g., Chapman Sq-dynamo null test)' if a readability EXP later bundles this with the paragraph-split Hole 3.

holes_found.description: Citation-by-our-tracker brittleness: 'PRED-095 as registered in data/predictions.json' cites *our* tracker file, not the dome's source of registration. An adversarial defender could respond 'data/predictions.json is your own data file; absence-of-field evidence from your tracker is not evidence of absence in my registration.' I verified by inspecting raw-text/03-predictions.txt that the dome's own predictions page confirms the claim — the dome publishes per-station amplitude bands and four named sub-tests, with no Chapman Sq-dynamo null-test language. The factual claim holds; only the citation is stylistically fragile. A belt-and-suspenders fix would co-cite the dome's own registration page (e.g., 'PRED-095 as registered at john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html (mirrored in data/predictions.json)' or 'PRED-095 as registered on the dome's predictions page, mirrored in data/predictions.json').
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish: add co-cite to the dome's own predictions page alongside data/predictions.json so the absence-of-Chapman-language claim is sourced to the dome's authoritative surface, not our mirror. Not blocking.

holes_found.description:
Carry-forward from c5: the L3a 'sub-tests, goalpost scope, Fail-clause double-bind' paragraph now runs ~402 words in a single

(down slightly from the ~600 c5 estimate after EXP-230's substring replacements, still above the section's other paragraphs). c5 Hole 3 explicitly flagged this structural split as needing its own EXP; EXP-230 correctly scoped that out. Tracking as a carry-forward minor so it does not get lost.
holes_found.recommendation:
No action from this review; follow the plan in EXP-230.out_of_scope_deliberately and c5 stronger_arguments — a separate structural EXP should split the paragraph at 'The dome also publishes four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests' so the four-sub-test enumeration, the goalpost-scope retort, the monitor.py scope-limit concession, and the Fail-clause double-bind each own their own

.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c5 stronger_arguments[2]: the upstream threshold-codification paragraph still contains 'globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author's own admission' (c4 Hole 2 globe-parasitism overreach). 'By the dome author's own admission' presupposes the dome author agrees PRED-095 is globe-parasitic; the dome's OPEN-001 admission is specifically about WGS84-dependent coordinates being in-progress to be replaced, not a concession that the overall prediction is globe-parasitic. EXP-230 did not touch this paragraph. Tracking as carry-forward minor so it does not get lost under EXP-230's closure of Hole 1.
holes_found.recommendation: No action from this review; scope for a future EXP narrowing 'by the dome author's own admission' to 'the dome author's own OPEN-001 admission that dome-native coordinates are IN PROGRESS' or similar. Not blocking.

stronger_arguments.description: The 'inherits the ambiguity below' burden-shift is the load-bearing rhetorical move of the rewrite, and it can be reinforced rather than just preserved. The strongest form of the argument is: 'The absence of a Chapman-language Fail criterion in PRED-095 as registered is not our problem to solve; the burden of specifying a discriminator is on the prediction author. In the absence of a dome-specified discriminator, an auditor codifies one from the nearest-neighbor null hypothesis (Chapman Sq-dynamo eclipse response, 1933, Matsushita 1965). That null hypothesis has exactly the two readings we give — baseline-Sq null test vs eclipse-modulated Chapman response — because they are the two standard ways ionospheric physicists decompose eclipse conductivity signatures. The ambiguity is a property of the null hypothesis, not of our wording.' This reframes the claim from 'any fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity' (vulnerable to E-PRED-B rejoinder) to 'any ionospheric-Sq-baseline null-test inherits the ambiguity' (scope-correct and defender-resistant).
sources: Chapman S. (1933) 'The solar tides, terrestrial currents and atmospheric tides' — historical Chapman Sq-dynamo formulation
Kim & Chang 2018 eclipse ensemble — the dome's own cited corpus

stronger_arguments.description: A potentially stronger framing of the attribution fix: the new sentence says 'the dome has not published any falsification criterion specific enough to disambiguate.' A sharper version cites what the dome *has* published (E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D sub-tests, magnitude bands with ±1-3 nT tolerances) and explicitly notes why they do not disambiguate at the magnitude-only cluster level. That moves the section from 'the dome failed to publish X' (which invites 'we published Y instead, is Y insufficient?') to 'the dome published Y which does not discriminate the magnitude-only cluster from Chapman-Sq, and our Fail-clause exists because Y leaves that gap.' Same conclusion, smaller defender surface.
sources: data/predictions.json PRED-095 detail_reasoning (our prior assessment language)

stronger_arguments.description: The 'monitor.py KAPPA-squared produces ~−29 nT, outside the W004 −17 to −21 nT cluster' observation in the middle paragraph is the section's sharpest factual hit but is buried. A dome defender cannot attack this without either (a) admitting the KAPPA² application was a bug, (b) admitting monitor.py is a separate internal tool with a separate formula family, or (c) abandoning the −17 to −21 nT cluster. Moving this observation closer to the double-bind would strengthen the 'formula selection among coexisting dome formulas' argument that currently sits in the 'goalpost scope' half of the paragraph. This is structural polish for a future EXP, not a correction to EXP-230.
sources: raw-text/monitor.py (KAPPA 1.672 applied twice in BOU 2017 formula, per WIN-025 three-formula audit)
raw-text/03-predictions.txt (W004 empirical −22.24 nT baseline vs −17 to −21 nT cluster)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author's fair-auditor proposal to structure the eclipse claim as a layered stack (anchor → timing → falsifier → caveat) is genuinely good methodology. Layered claims force explicit statements about what would falsify each layer, and spelling out a Tier 3 threshold is due diligence. The dome's E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D sub-tests also *are* explicitly mechanism-discriminating — E-PRED-B (Hartland beats Ebro despite lower coverage) is a direct V12-vs-V9 discriminator that cannot be rationalized as a Chapman-Sq-dynamo expectation. Under the narrow magnitude-only-W004 framing the section targets, those sub-tests do not save the Pass band from the non-discrimination critique, but they are real and good-faith.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The layered presentation and E-PRED-A-through-D sub-tests do not repair the specific structural flaws the section identifies: (1) an ensemble 'anchor' fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor — layering does not change that; (2) the magnitude-only W004 Pass band remains non-discriminating regardless of whether the sub-tests are evaluated separately; (3) PRED-095's dome-derivation path remains globe-parasitic via WIN-025-documented INTERMAGNET baselines and WGS84-dependent FSF coordinates, independent of whether E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D succeed or fail. The sub-tests can pass or fail without affecting the section's core argument; they are evaluated separately in Section 4.2 and Section 9, and EXP-230 correctly preserves that scope separation.

deeper_analysis:
EXP-230 is a tightly scoped attribution fix and lands cleanly. The before/after substrings match the patches declared in monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-230-SEC8b.3-attribution.json, and both replacements preserved the surrounding interpretation-A / interpretation-B clauses and the forced-disjunction conclusion. c5 Hole 1 (moderate, attribution misframing) is closed: the Fail-clause is now explicitly owned as 'Our codified Fail-clause … that we appended to our Pass band,' and the disambiguation burden is rerouted via 'the dome has not published any falsification criterion specific enough to disambiguate' plus the parenthetical citing PRED-095 as registered. The Patch B swap ('The dome has not committed to either disambiguation' in place of 'The dome does not publish which reading governs') is a clean semantic upgrade: the new phrasing does not presuppose the dome is the original author.

The new attack surface introduced by Patch A is the unscoped 'so any fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below' parenthetical. 'Any' here is over-strong. The dome's own raw-text/03-predictions.txt publishes four named model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A/B/C/D) that are precisely the kind of non-ambiguous Fail-clauses a fair auditor could adopt. E-PRED-B (Hartland peak beats Ebro peak despite lower coverage) is a directional test that is immune to the baseline-Sq vs Chapman-Sq-dynamo reading ambiguity: the observables are peak-over-peak ratios at specific stations, not amplitude bands interpreted against an ionospheric baseline. A defender reading only the double-bind paragraph will see 'any' as overreach. The section's own upstream text concedes the sub-tests exist ('The dome also publishes four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests') and scope-limits them ('do not change the non-discrimination assessment of the magnitude-only W004 cluster'), but the new parenthetical does not carry that scope-limit forward. A one-word scope-narrowing fix ('any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause' or 'any fair-audit Fail-clause framed against the ionospheric Sq baseline') resolves this cleanly and preserves the force of the double-bind within its correct scope.

The semantic slip in 'appended to our Pass band' is minor — the clause was included in the codification from the outset per the upstream paragraph, not added later — but a precise defender could use the word 'appended' to argue that the ambiguity was bolted on and is removable by deletion. 'Included in' or 'embedded within' would foreclose that rejoinder.

The citation-by-our-tracker fragility ('PRED-095 as registered in data/predictions.json') is real but corroborated: raw-text/03-predictions.txt confirms the dome's predictions page publishes per-station amplitude bands and E-PRED-A/B/C/D but no Chapman Sq-dynamo null-test. A co-cite to the dome's authoritative page is optional polish.

None of these findings overturn the rewrite. The rewrite successfully closes the ISS-1422 attribution hole that c5 identified as the strongest single defender move on the section. Current verdict (adopted-as-steel-man) continues to hold, and the recommended_action is minor_edit: the one-word scope fix to 'any fair-audit Fail-clause' is the only moderate-severity item; everything else is carry-forward or stylistic polish.

Cross-WIN consistency: EXP-230's propagation_note flagged possible similar attribution misframing in SEC-8b.1 (claim_key annotations) and SEC-8b.2 (sequenced-read table). I checked both. SEC-8b.1 does not impose a fair-audit Fail-clause at all — it adopts claim_key annotations and retains the 'API disambiguates, display does not' finding without a null-test codification. SEC-8b.2 labels each link in the sequenced-read chain with a verdict (Row 1 Misleading, etc.), but the verdicts are our review's verdicts of the dome's claim, not fair-audit Fail-clauses we are imposing. Neither section carries the attribution misframing pattern EXP-230 repaired. The propagation check surfaces no new EXPs from this angle.

Cycle 1/2 (pre-V6 restructure) reviews of this section lived under the 'Part 4.6' old numbering; c2 (Apr 20), c3 (Apr 21), c4 (Apr 22), c5 (Apr 22) are post-restructure. The v6-restructure-map confirms part4c→part8b. No old-numbering translations surface new findings.
